pi_items.source: Task description: 'Save the file to /home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/box_dieline.dxf'. Confirmed in setup_task.sh and export_result.sh scripts.
Task description specifies 'create layer CUT (Color: Red, Linetype: Continuous)'. Verified in verifier.py which checks for 'CUT' layer.
DXF/AutoCAD color standard: color code 1 = RED (verified via ezdxf documentation and AutoCAD DXF Reference). Task description confirms 'CUT layer (Color: Red)'.
Task description specifies 'create layer FOLD (Color: Blue, Linetype: Dashed)'. Verified in verifier.py which checks for 'FOLD' layer.
DXF/AutoCAD color standard: color code 5 = BLUE (verified via ezdxf documentation and AutoCAD DXF Reference). Task description confirms 'FOLD layer (Color: Blue)'.
Task description: '4 main panels, each 60mm wide x 100mm high'. Metadata value matches the specified panel width.
Task description: '4 main panels, each 60mm wide x 100mm high'. Metadata value matches the specified panel height.
Task description: 'Top and Bottom flaps extending 30mm from the main body'. Metadata value matches the specified flap extension height.

privileged_info_summary: All metadata claims are verified. The task creates a synthetic DXF file (box_dieline.dxf) containing a packaging box die-cut net with specified dimensions: 60mm wide x 100mm high main panels, 30mm flaps, and a 15mm glue tab. Two layers must be created: CUT layer (DXF color code 1 = RED) with continuous lines for cut edges, and FOLD layer (DXF color code 5 = BLUE) with dashed lines for crease/fold lines. The glue tab must have chamfered corners. All DXF color codes and dimension specifications are consistent with industry standard and match the task requirements.
